DELETE_CANDIDATES.reason: Only used in test file. Pipeline engine was planned but never integrated into Stateflow V4.
DELETE_CANDIDATES.justification: Pipeline engine was designed for YAML-based workflow but never integrated. Stateflow V4 uses direct file_detect.py and automation flows. Test file should also be deleted.

DELETE_CANDIDATES.reason: Zero imports and zero usage - dead code. EMR was replaced by file_detect.py in Stateflow V4. Only referenced in reset_event_tables.py for database filename, not actual import.
DELETE_CANDIDATES.justification: Episode Metadata Registry was a transitional module. Stateflow V4 uses file_detect.py for all asset state detection. Database file reference in reset_event_tables.py is just a filename string, not an import.

DELETE_CANDIDATES.justification: Task priority system was designed for multi-dimensional priority calculation but never integrated into render_queue or upload_queue. Uses setattr/getattr internally but module itself is never imported.

DELETE_CANDIDATES.reason: Zero imports and zero usage - dead code. Cleanup routes were deleted in Phase 5, service is orphaned.
DELETE_CANDIDATES.justification: cleanup.py route was deleted in Phase 5 Dead Code Cleanup. cleanup_service.py is now orphaned with no routes using it. Only referenced in validate_no_asr_left.py whitelist (for directory checks).

RETAIN_LIST.reason: CLI script - may be called directly via command line.
RETAIN_LIST.action: Keep as CLI tool - scripts are called directly, not imported
RETAIN_LIST.note: CLI scripts are executed directly, not imported. This is expected behavior.

RETAIN_LIST.reason: Protected script - used by CI/CD and governance validation.
RETAIN_LIST.action: Keep - protected script from governance document
RETAIN_LIST.note: This script is part of the guardrail system established in Phase 4-P5.

RISK_FILES.risk: CLI script - may be called directly
RISK_FILES.recommendation: Keep if used as CLI tool, otherwise can be deleted.

notes.import_count_limitation: Import count of 0 may be misleading for files that are used via dynamic imports, plugin systems, or direct execution (scripts). Actual usage was verified via codebase search.
notes.protected_modules: All files in PROTECTED_MODULES list from governance document are automatically classified as Category C (Retain).
notes.plugin_system: Plugins are loaded dynamically by plugin_system.py, so they may show 0 imports but are still used.
notes.scripts: CLI scripts are executed directly, not imported, so import_count=0 is expected.
